articles.title: US strikes Iran after Trump calls Strait of Hormuz drone attack a 'foolish violation' of ceasefire
articles.summary: US Central Command said its aircraft struck Iranian missile and drone storage locations and coastal radar sites on June 26, calling it a 'powerful response' to Tehran's 'dangerous behavior' a day after an IRGC drone hit a Singapore-flagged cargo ship (Evergreen's Ever Lovely) off Oman in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump accused Iran of a 'foolish violation' of the 60-day ceasefire, saying it fired at least four one-way attack drones at vessels, one of which struck the upper deck of the ship while three others were shot down. Vice President JD Vance said 'violence will be met with violence,' adding that Washington had honored the memorandum of understanding and that Iran could use the agreed deconfliction line if it had grievances. The exchange marks the most serious test of the US-Iran deal signed June 17.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon sign US-brokered framework deal for partial IDF withdrawal
articles.summary: After four days of talks in Washington, Israel and Lebanon signed a trilateral framework agreement under which the IDF will partially withdraw from two pilot zones in southern Lebanon, with the Lebanese Armed Forces deploying in their place. The deal creates a US-facilitated Military Coordination Group for Lebanon (MCG4L) to oversee implementation, which Secretary of State Marco Rubio called 'the beginning of the beginning' toward restoring Lebanese sovereignty, disarming Hezbollah and dismantling its infrastructure. Israeli officials said there is no fixed timetable and that Israel will keep its buffer zone until the Lebanese army demonstrates it can dismantle Hezbollah; Netanyahu said troops will remain 'until Hezbollah disarms.' Hezbollah and its backers view the limited pullback as falling short of the permanent end to operations required by the June 17 MOU.

articles.title: Grossi says US-Iran deal grants IAEA access to nuclear sites; Tehran disputes it
articles.summary: Speaking at a press conference in Japan on June 26, IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi said the memorandum of understanding between Washington and Tehran includes access for UN inspectors to Iran's nuclear facilities, and called for a 'very strong system of verification' to ensure Iran cannot build a weapon. Grossi said inspectors had already held initial technical discussions with Iranian officials. Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi countered that there are no immediate plans to grant access to key sites until sanctions are lifted and a final agreement is concluded, exposing a direct contradiction over how the ceasefire terms should be implemented.

articles.title: Iran signals it will resume Strait of Hormuz transit fees after 60-day pause
articles.summary: A member of Iran's negotiating team indicated Tehran intends to resume collecting fees from vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz once the 60-day suspension agreed in the ceasefire expires, saying Iran accepted the temporary pause only 'so that the agreement could be reached.' Separately, Omani authorities told European officials there is 'no way of going back to the pre-war status quo' in the strait and that ships may face charges for de-pollution and navigation assistance. Gulf states and the US have rejected any Iranian tolls on the waterway, through which roughly a fifth of the world's oil passed before the war, keeping the question of who controls Hormuz traffic a central sticking point.

articles.title: Palestinian man shot dead by Israeli troops in West Bank as settler attacks surge
articles.summary: A Palestinian man was shot dead by Israeli troops in the occupied West Bank on June 26, hours before his son was born, amid a sharp rise in raids and settler violence that the UN now estimates at around six settler attacks per day. In Atuf, southeast of Tubas, settlers reportedly wearing Israeli military uniforms assaulted and abducted a group of Palestinian farmers, while an infant died of wounds from a shooting in al-Khalil (Hebron). Israeli prosecutors separately charged six settlers over an attack on a West Bank mosque, as rights groups report escalating demolitions and assaults across the territory.

articles.title: Israeli airstrike hits Nabatieh outskirts during Ashoura despite fragile ceasefire
articles.summary: An Israeli airstrike struck the outskirts of Nabatieh in southern Lebanon on June 26 as residents marked Ashoura, the latest in a series of attacks that have continued through the fragile Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire. Israel says its operations target Hezbollah operatives and infrastructure, while Lebanese authorities and the Iran-backed group describe the strikes as repeated ceasefire violations. The strike landed on the same day Israel and Lebanon signed their framework withdrawal deal in Washington, underscoring the gap between the diplomatic track and conditions on the ground.

articles.title: Israeli forces to stay in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria 'without time limit', Katz says, as Gaza aid hub is overhauled
articles.summary: Defense Minister Israel Katz reiterated that Israeli forces will remain inside security zones in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria 'without any time limit' to guard against what he called jihadist threats, comments echoed by an Israeli envoy who said any IDF withdrawal from Lebanon is tied to Hezbollah disarmament rather than a fixed timetable. The remarks came as the Civil-Military Coordination Center in Kiryat Gat is being overhauled and renamed the International Gaza Aid Center, tied more closely to the US-backed Board of Peace overseeing the Gaza ceasefire, aid entry and postwar planning, with Georgia and Vietnam reported to be in talks to contribute troops to a planned International Stabilization Force.
